reasoning:
San Diego FC has started their inaugural season incredibly well, securing 11 points in their first 6 matches (3W, 2D, 1L). They have defeated top-tier Western Conference opponents including the LA Galaxy, Real Salt Lake, and recently LAFC on March 29. Given that 9 out of 15 teams in the Western Conference make the playoffs, the standard cutoff is usually around 40-43 points. With 11 points already secured, San Diego only needs to average roughly 1.10 - 1.15 points per game over their remaining 28 fixtures to qualify. 

The market traded at 41% around March 20 (when they were at 8 points through 4 matches). This suggests significant early skepticism about their long-term depth and ability to sustain their form throughout a demanding 34-game MLS season, a common issue for expansion sides. Furthermore, star player Hirving Lozano recently dealt with a minor hamstring issue. 

However, beating LAFC on March 29 (and scoring 3 goals quickly) validates that their early wins were not flukes and that they can field a competitive lineup even when managing injuries and international absences. Taking into account the large points cushion they've built up while respecting the market's traditional expansion-discount, I estimate their probability of qualifying sits moderately above a coin flip.
